concepts.finding:
In order to achieve state-of-the-art results, recent
methods narrow the search space to architectures composed of basic building blocks with strong
domain priors such as CNNs [70, 78, 95, 128], recurrent cells [53, 78, 128] and self-attention [107].
concepts.implication: Architecture pattern applicable to sequence modeling for user behavior

concepts.finding:
As a proof of concept, we also apply our search method on a
supervised learning domain, and ﬁnd it can discover networks that, even without explicit weight
training, can achieve a much higher than chance test accuracy of ∼92% on MNIST.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
It has been shown that random search can already achieve SOTA results if such priors are used [69,
94, 104].
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
The inner loop for training the weights of each candidate architecture before evaluation
makes the search costly, although efforts have been made to improve efﬁciency [10, 71, 91].
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

[85] proposed variance networks, which sample each weight from a distribution
with a zero mean and a learned variance parameter, and show that ensemble evaluations can improve
performance on image recognition tasks.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
3
Weight Agnostic Neural Network Search
Creating network architectures which encode solutions is a fundamentally different problem than
that addressed by neural architecture search (NAS).
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
By formulating the search as a multi-objective optimization problem [57, 83] we take into
account the size of the network as well as its performance when ranking it in the population.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

5
Discussion and Future Work
In this work we introduced a method to search for simple neural network architectures with strong
inductive biases.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
For instance, effectively training models that rely on discrete
components [32, 52] or utilize adaptive computation mechanisms [31] with gradient-based methods
remain a challenging research area.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding: Exploiting open-endedness to solve problems through the search for novelty.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
We propose a search method for neural
network architectures that can already perform a task without any explicit weight
training.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
We demonstrate that our method can ﬁnd minimal neural
network architectures that can perform several reinforcement learning tasks without
weight training.
concepts.implication: Can optimize long-term user engagement metrics

concepts.finding:
On a supervised learning domain, we ﬁnd network architectures
that achieve much higher than chance accuracy on MNIST using random weights.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

arXiv:1906.04358v2  [cs.LG]  5 Sep 2019

Decades of neural network research have provided building blocks with strong inductive biases for
various task domains.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Recent work [45, 117] demonstrated that even randomly-initialized CNNs can be used effectively
for image processing tasks such as superresolution, inpainting and style transfer.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
In order to ﬁnd neural network architectures with strong inductive biases, we propose to search for
architectures by deemphasizing the importance of weights.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
We demonstrate
our approach can produce networks that can be expected to perform various continuous control tasks
with a random weight parameter.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Our method is
based on NEAT [110], an established topology search algorithm notable for its ability to optimize the
weights and structure of networks simultaneously.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Ideas related to MDL
for making neural networks “simple” was proposed in the 1990s, such as simplifying networks by
soft-weight sharing [86], reducing the amount of information in weights by making them noisy [47],
and simplifying the search space of its weights [102].
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization
